ranges.label: Low Difficulty
ranges.description: Your emotion regulation abilities are well-developed. You generally manage intense feelings effectively, maintain behavioral control during distress, and recover from emotional upsets without significant disruption to daily functioning.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your effective emotion regulation skills through regular self-reflection
Your emotional resilience is a valuable asset; keep nurturing it through healthy habits
Consider sharing your coping strategies with others who may benefit from your experience

ranges.label: Moderate Difficulty
ranges.description: You experience moderate difficulty regulating emotions. Intense feelings may occasionally overwhelm your coping resources, leading to temporary disruptions in concentration, goal-directed behavior, or interpersonal interactions.
suggestions: Practice mindfulness meditation regularly to increase awareness of emotional states as they arise
Learn cognitive reappraisal strategies to reframe distressing situations more constructively
Regular aerobic exercise has strong evidence for improving emotional regulation capacity
Consider journaling daily to identify patterns in your emotional triggers and reactions

ranges.label: High Difficulty
ranges.description: You have high difficulty managing emotions. Intense emotional episodes may frequently interfere with your ability to complete tasks, maintain relationships, and sustain psychological well-being across multiple life domains.
suggestions: Consulting a therapist trained in emotion regulation approaches is recommended at this level
Dialectical Behavior Therapy skills for distress tolerance and emotion regulation can be highly effective
Practice grounding techniques such as the 5-4-3-2-1 sensory exercise during emotional overwhelm
Build a reliable support network of people you can reach out to when feeling emotionally overwhelmed
Develop a personalized emotion regulation plan with both preventive and in-the-moment strategies

ranges.label: Very High Difficulty
ranges.description: You report very high difficulty with emotion regulation. This degree of dysregulation likely has substantial negative impact on your relationships, occupational functioning, self-image, and overall quality of life.
suggestions: Seeking professional therapy from a licensed mental health provider is strongly recommended
Dialectical Behavior Therapy or emotion-focused therapy can produce significant improvements
Develop a safety plan with a therapist for managing episodes of extreme emotional distress
Consistent, ongoing therapeutic support offers the best path toward meaningful and lasting improvement

clarity.label: Average Clarity
clarity.description: Sometimes difficulty making sense of feelings.

goals.label: Moderate Impact
goals.description: Emotions sometimes interfere with getting things done.

goals.label: High Impact
goals.description: Emotions severely impair task completion and focus.

impulse.label: Average Control
impulse.description: Sometimes difficulty controlling behaviors when upset.

impulse.label: Poor Control
impulse.description: Significant difficulty controlling behavior when emotionally upset.

nonacceptance.label: High Acceptance
nonacceptance.description: Can accept emotional reactions without self-judgment.

nonacceptance.label: Low Acceptance
nonacceptance.description: Strong secondary negative reactions to own emotions.

strategies.label: Rich Strategies
strategies.description: Multiple effective emotion regulation strategies available.

strategies.label: Average Strategies
strategies.description: Some strategies available but may feel stuck at times.

strategies.label: Limited Strategies
strategies.description: Lacks effective coping strategies when emotionally distressed.

ranges.label: Normal Range
ranges.description: Your dissociative experiences fall within the normal range. Most people occasionally experience brief moments of absorption, daydreaming, or automatic behavior that are entirely typical and not clinically significant.
suggestions: These occasional dissociative experiences are a normal part of human cognition and attention
If certain experiences feel bothersome, note when and where they occur for self-awareness
Maintaining good sleep hygiene and stress management supports continued mental clarity

ranges.label: Mild Dissociative Experiences
ranges.description: Your dissociative experiences are mildly elevated above the population average. This may relate to ongoing stress, sleep deprivation, or anxiety, and warrants some self-monitoring to track changes over time.
suggestions: Monitor your stress levels and sleep quality, as both strongly influence dissociative experiences
Practice grounding and mindfulness exercises to strengthen present-moment awareness daily
Identify situations or triggers that seem to precede dissociative episodes for better self-understanding
If these experiences are increasing or affecting daily functioning, consult a mental health professional

ranges.label: Moderate Dissociative Experiences
ranges.description: Your dissociative experience frequency is clinically elevated and may indicate a dissociative disorder or related condition such as PTSD. A comprehensive professional evaluation is recommended to clarify the pattern.
suggestions: A professional evaluation by a clinician experienced with dissociative disorders is recommended
Learn about dissociative symptoms to better understand and communicate your experiences to others
Practice grounding techniques such as the 5-4-3-2-1 sensory exercise when feeling disconnected
Maintain regular daily routines and minimize controllable stressors to reduce dissociative episodes
Consider trauma-informed therapy approaches if your dissociation is linked to past traumatic events

ranges.label: Significant Dissociative Experiences
ranges.description: You report frequent and significant dissociative experiences. Scores at this level are strongly associated with dissociative disorders, complex PTSD, and related conditions requiring comprehensive clinical assessment.
suggestions: A thorough psychiatric or psychological evaluation by a dissociation specialist is strongly recommended
Dissociation often has roots in early traumatic experiences; trauma-focused therapy can be very effective
Develop a safety plan with your clinician for managing severe dissociative episodes in daily life
Do not underestimate the impact of these experiences on your safety, relationships, and daily functioning

amnesia.label: High
amnesia.description: Frequent amnesia-related dissociative experiences. Attention recommended.

depersonalization.label: High
depersonalization.description: Frequent depersonalization/derealization experiences. Professional evaluation recommended.

absorption.label: Moderate
absorption.description: Some absorption or imaginative involvement experiences.

ranges.label: Low Procrastination
ranges.description: Your procrastination tendency is low. You demonstrate strong self-regulation, consistently initiate tasks in a timely manner, and manage deadlines effectively across personal and professional responsibilities.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your excellent time management and self-discipline habits
Consider mentoring others who struggle with procrastination using your effective strategies
Stay mindful during high-stress periods when even disciplined individuals may slip temporarily

ranges.label: Moderate Procrastination
ranges.description: You show a moderate procrastination pattern that occasionally delays task completion and reduces productivity. While not severely impairing, this tendency may cause unnecessary stress and missed opportunities.
suggestions: Break large or overwhelming tasks into smaller, concrete action steps with specific deadlines
Use the Pomodoro Technique or similar time-blocking methods to sustain focused work sessions
Set personal deadlines that precede actual deadlines to create a buffer against delays
Reduce environmental distractions by creating a dedicated, organized workspace for focused tasks

ranges.label: High Procrastination
ranges.description: Your procrastination pattern is pronounced and likely affects your work performance, academic outcomes, and daily responsibilities on a regular basis. Chronic delay may erode self-confidence and increase anxiety.
suggestions: Use task management tools such as structured to-do lists or digital planners to track commitments
Find an accountability partner who can provide regular check-ins and mutual encouragement
Explore the psychological drivers behind your procrastination, such as perfectionism or fear of failure
Consider cognitive-behavioral counseling focused specifically on procrastination and avoidance patterns
Practice self-compassion rather than self-criticism when you notice procrastination tendencies arising

ranges.label: Severe Procrastination
ranges.description: Your procrastination is severe and pervasive. This level of chronic avoidance likely causes significant harm to your career, relationships, finances, and self-esteem, warranting professional intervention.
suggestions: Seeking help from a mental health professional experienced with procrastination is strongly recommended
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy has demonstrated strong effectiveness for chronic procrastination patterns
Start with the smallest possible task step to gradually rebuild your confidence in task completion
Address the emotional burden of procrastination with self-compassion and structured professional support

ranges.label: No Hoarding Problems
ranges.description: Your hoarding behaviors fall within the normal range. You demonstrate typical patterns of acquiring, organizing, and discarding possessions without significant difficulty or distress related to these activities.
suggestions: Maintain your regular habits of organizing and periodically reviewing belongings
Mild reluctance to discard certain sentimental items is entirely normal and expected
Continue practicing thoughtful acquisition habits to prevent future accumulation issues

ranges.label: Mild Hoarding
ranges.description: You show mild hoarding tendencies including some difficulty discarding items or mild clutter. While not yet significantly impairing daily life, these patterns may gradually worsen without proactive management.
suggestions: Establish regular decluttering routines, such as reviewing one area of your home each week
Before purchasing items, pause and evaluate whether you genuinely need and will use them
Practice the one-in-one-out rule: remove an existing item whenever you acquire something new
If difficulties increase over time, consider consulting a professional before patterns solidify

ranges.label: Moderate Hoarding
ranges.description: Your hoarding behavior has reached clinically significant levels. Clutter, difficulty discarding, or excessive acquisition likely affects your living space, daily routines, and may cause notable emotional distress.
suggestions: Consulting a mental health professional experienced with hoarding disorder is recommended
Cognitive Behavioral Therapy specifically adapted for hoarding has strong evidence of effectiveness
Address clutter gradually by clearing small areas rather than attempting overwhelming large-scale cleanouts
Educate yourself about hoarding disorder to better understand the psychological mechanisms involved
Seek understanding and practical support from trusted family members or friends during the process

ranges.label: Severe Hoarding
ranges.description: You report severe hoarding problems that substantially impact your quality of life, physical safety, and social functioning. Professional intervention is strongly recommended to address these significant challenges.
suggestions: Consulting a psychiatrist or hoarding disorder specialist therapist is strongly recommended
Hoarding disorder is a recognized, treatable condition with effective professional interventions available
Do not allow shame or embarrassment to prevent you from seeking the help you deserve
Medication combined with specialized CBT may provide the most comprehensive treatment approach
Prioritize basic safety in your living environment while working on longer-term therapeutic goals

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your gaming behavior falls within the normal range with no indicators of Internet Gaming Disorder. Gaming appears to be a healthy recreational activity that does not interfere with your responsibilities or well-being.
suggestions: Continue maintaining healthy and balanced gaming habits alongside other life activities
Stay aware of your gaming time and its impact on sleep, work, and social relationships
Model healthy gaming behavior for peers and younger family members in your environment

ranges.label: At Risk
ranges.description: You show some patterns of problematic gaming behavior that place you at risk for developing Internet Gaming Disorder. Gaming may occasionally interfere with sleep, responsibilities, or social engagement.
suggestions: Set clear daily time limits for gaming sessions and use built-in parental or timer controls
Actively develop and invest in hobbies and social activities that do not involve gaming
Monitor the impact of gaming on your sleep quality, academic or work performance, and relationships
If you find it increasingly difficult to control gaming time, consider consulting a professional early

ranges.label: High Risk
ranges.description: Your gaming behavior has noticeably impacted multiple areas of daily life including social relationships, academic or occupational performance, and physical health. Professional guidance is recommended at this level.
suggestions: Consulting a mental health professional experienced with behavioral addictions is recommended
Gradually reduce gaming time using a structured schedule rather than attempting abrupt cessation
Identify and develop alternative sources of relaxation, achievement, and social connection offline
Openly discuss your gaming habits with someone you trust for external perspective and accountability
Address underlying emotional needs that gaming may be fulfilling, such as stress relief or escapism

ranges.label: Probable Gaming Disorder
ranges.description: Your score suggests probable Internet Gaming Disorder. Gaming has severely affected your daily functioning, relationships, and well-being, meeting criteria that warrant immediate professional clinical attention.
suggestions: Seeking evaluation from a psychiatrist or psychotherapist specializing in gaming addiction is urgent
Professional behavioral intervention therapy can effectively address compulsive gaming patterns
Build a support system by informing family and close friends about the severity of the situation
Consider environmental controls such as limiting unsupervised access to gaming devices and accounts

ranges.label: Low Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindful attention awareness is low, indicating that you frequently operate on autopilot during daily activities. Reduced present-moment awareness is associated with higher stress reactivity and lower well-being.
suggestions: Begin with just 5 minutes of guided mindfulness meditation daily and gradually increase duration
Practice paying deliberate, focused attention during routine activities such as eating and walking
Reduce habitual multitasking, which fragments attention and reinforces automatic pilot behavior
Consider enrolling in a structured Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction program for guided training

ranges.label: Moderate Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindful attention awareness is moderate. You sometimes maintain present-moment awareness but frequently shift into automatic processing. Building consistency in mindfulness practice can yield meaningful benefits.
suggestions: Increase the frequency and duration of your mindfulness practice to build sustained awareness
Extend mindful awareness to more daily activities including conversations, meals, and commutes
Experiment with different mindfulness techniques such as mindful eating, walking, or body scanning
Notice when your mind wanders and gently redirect attention without self-judgment or frustration
Consider joining a mindfulness group or community for shared practice and mutual encouragement

ranges.label: High Mindfulness
ranges.description: Your mindful attention awareness is high. You generally maintain strong present-moment awareness during daily activities, which is associated with greater emotional regulation, reduced stress, and enhanced well-being.
suggestions: Continue and deepen your existing mindfulness practice routine to sustain these benefits
Explore advanced mindfulness techniques such as loving-kindness or open awareness meditation
Integrate mindful awareness into challenging situations where maintaining presence is most difficult

ranges.label: Low Narcissism
ranges.description: Your score indicates low narcissistic traits. You tend toward modesty, may undervalue your own qualities, and generally prioritize others over self-promotion in social interactions and relationships.
suggestions: While modesty is admirable, ensure you also recognize and appreciate your genuine strengths
Practice healthy self-assertion and advocacy when your needs or contributions deserve recognition
Balance your natural humility with confidence in the abilities and qualities you genuinely possess

ranges.label: Below Average
ranges.description: Your narcissism score is below average, reflecting a healthy balance of self-regard and consideration for others. You demonstrate appropriate self-confidence without excessive self-focus or entitlement patterns.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your balanced and grounded perspective on yourself and your accomplishments
Your healthy self-awareness and interpersonal sensitivity are positive qualities worth preserving
Feel comfortable acknowledging your achievements openly without concern about appearing boastful

ranges.label: Average
ranges.description: Your narcissism score falls within the average range for the general population. This reflects typical levels of self-confidence, ambition, and desire for recognition that most people experience.
suggestions: Your level of self-regard is well within the normal and healthy range for most adults
Continue balancing healthy confidence and ambition with genuine empathy and concern for others
Remain aware of how your self-confidence is perceived by colleagues, friends, and family members

ranges.label: Above Average
ranges.description: Your score is above average, suggesting elevated narcissistic traits including heightened self-importance, stronger need for admiration, and potentially reduced sensitivity to the perspectives of others.
suggestions: Practice active listening by focusing on understanding others before sharing your own perspective
Seek honest, constructive feedback from people you trust about how your behavior affects them
Reflect regularly on how your actions and communication style may impact those around you
Examine whether your expectations of special treatment or admiration from others are proportionate
Consider developing deeper empathy through volunteering or perspective-taking exercises

ranges.label: High Narcissism
ranges.description: Your score indicates a high level of narcissistic traits including strong feelings of superiority, pronounced need for admiration, sense of entitlement, and potential difficulty with genuine empathy.
suggestions: Reflect carefully on your close relationships and honestly assess how others respond to your behavior
Practice perspective-taking by genuinely considering situations from the viewpoint of other people
A counselor or therapist can help you explore these traits in a supportive, non-judgmental setting
Work on building genuine reciprocity and mutual respect in your personal and professional relationships
